title: nie-daemon web: embedded index.html + JavaScript client
description:
**What:** Create daemon/web/index.html (3-panel layout: users, chat, wallet stub) with inline JavaScript that connects to /ws/events, fetches /api/whoami + /api/users on load, and sends messages via POST /api/send.

**Algorithm:**
1. Create daemon/web/ directory
2. Write daemon/web/index.html (single file, no external deps, all inline):
   a. HTML skeleton: three-column layout — left panel (users list), center panel (chat log + text input + send button), right panel (wallet stub showing 'Wallet coming in Phase 2')
   b. CSS: minimal flexbox layout, dark-ish theme, monospace font for pub_ids
   c. JavaScript:
      - const TOKEN_KEY = 'nie_daemon_token';
      - On DOMContentLoaded: read token from localStorage; if missing, prompt user 'Enter daemon token:'; store in localStorage
      - Fetch /api/whoami (Authorization: Bearer {token}) → display own pub_id in status bar
      - Fetch /api/users → render users in left panel (online/offline)
      - Connect WebSocket ws://localhost:7734/ws/events with Authorization header via query param ?token={token} if browsers don't allow WS auth headers, OR use a ws-ticket approach: POST /api/ws-ticket → get one-time token → connect ws://localhost:7734/ws/events?ticket={ticket}
      - SIMPLER APPROACH: token in URL fragment, server reads it from query string on WS upgrade. Implement as query param ?token=... on the WS URL; update WS handler to also accept ?token= query param
      - WS onmessage: parse JSON; switch on .type: 'message_received' → append div to chat log; 'user_joined'/'user_left'/'directory_updated' → refresh users panel
      - Send button / Enter key: POST /api/send { text } → append optimistic message to chat, show error if 4xx/5xx
      - On 401 from any request: show re-enter token prompt
3. Create daemon/src/web.rs:
   - const INDEX_HTML: &str = include_str!('../web/index.html');
   - pub async fn handle_index() -> impl IntoResponse { ([(axum::http::header::CONTENT_TYPE, 'text/html; charset=utf-8')], INDEX_HTML) }
4. Update WS handler (nie-0tvc.6 / W2-C) to accept token from query param: extract ?token= from WS URL as fallback if no Authorization header
5. Register routes in main.rs: .route('/', get(web::handle_index)), .route('/index.html', get(web::handle_index))
6. Note: GET / does NOT require auth (token entered in browser — user sees the page first)

**Defensive programming rules:**
- Token not embedded in HTML (it changes and is user-specific)
- No external CDN resources in HTML — no <script src='https://...'>
- Token stored in localStorage only (not URL bar, not cookies)
- All API calls include Authorization header
- No inline eval() or unsafe JS patterns

**Agent team:** Implementer (HTML+JS, no research needed). Validator (cargo build + visual spot check).

**Tests:**
- test_static_serve: GET http://127.0.0.1:7734/ returns 200 with Content-Type: text/html
- test_index_html_no_external_resources: grep for 'https://' in index.html → must find 0 matches (no CDN)
acceptance_criteria: GET / returns 200 text/html; no external resource URLs in HTML; cargo clippy -- -D warnings exits 0; page renders in browser with 3 panels
close_reason: embedded web client implemented; HTML tests pass
